programmes.show_description: Bifocal comedy with Messrs Barker and Corbett, featuring the sketches All Change, Conversation Piece and Language Barrier.
Another burst of bifocal comedy with the much-missed duo of Messrs Barker and Corbett. This time, the sketches include Coke Up, Sid and Lil, Bear Facts and a musical finale.
Messrs Barker and Corbett offer up more bifocal comedy with witty wordplay and, of course, a delightfully digressive anecdote from Mr Corbett.
Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker offer up their special blend of spoof news items, meandering anecdotes, and brilliantly inventive sketches.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: How to Build a London Cab: With factory access, see how a 21st century electric London Taxi is made in 28 hours.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: Mercedes S Class: Factory 56 opens its doors as we follow the ultimate configuration of a Mercedes-Benz S-Class, from the chassis build to the main assembly floor.
Shopping from Home.
Yorkshire's HART paramedics race to help a man who has been stabbed in the back with a screwdriver, and there's a fire in a hotel resulting in a desperate search for the people inside.
A man is left with a metal spike impaled in his face after a traumatic incident on a construction site, sparking a huge rescue operation. Plus, medics help a man who was hit by a car.
It's Not All Beaches and Blue Skies: Anna hits the first milestone in Australia without the support of family: Isabelle's 8th birthday. Jay settles into work, but life at home is not so smooth.

Shopping from Home.
Almost Feels Like Home: Maggie and Darren start to settle in at Perth's central station, but Anna struggles with her work-life balance. Michael gets a very unusual reptilian call out.
No Longer Rookies: The Khan family have embraced life in Perth so fully that they've decided to make it permanent by purchasing land, while Anna is struggling with the shift hours.
Policing in Paradise: It's the end of probation and Maggie's dream of being a police dog handler is revived, while Michael returns to Perth from Halls Creek. Jay and his wife make a decision.

The Doctor and Sarah Jane face a deadly alien conspiracy in a tense showdown that will decide the fate of Earth.
The Doctor grows suspicious when the Master, disguised as Professor Thascalos, conducts dangerous experiments in time manipulation at a research institute.
The Master's time experiments create strange temporal distortions, forcing the Doctor to build his own 'time sensor' to counter the growing threat.
The Master summons Kronos, the time-eating Chronovore, but loses control as the creature wreaks havoc, leaving both the Doctor and UNIT scrambling to contain it.
The Doctor and Jo pursue the Master through the Time Vortex in a dramatic 'TARDIS-within-TARDIS' chase that tests both their machines and their wits.
Both TARDISes arrive in ancient Atlantis, where the Master manipulates King Dalios and Queen Galleia to gain control over the crystal that binds Kronos.
